title: Obscurium: Tools + Modifiers
description:
Sugar Bytes Obscurium. Released 14th of July 2015.

http://www.sugar-bytes.de/content/products/Obscurium/index.php?lang=en

0:23 Draw Tools Intro
1:52 Draw All Button / Offset
2:12 Modifiers
2:32 The Lock Button
3:37 Move Modifier
4:10 Stretch/Shrink Modifier
4:38 Rotate Modifier
5:22 The ALL Button
5:43 Super Obscure Mode
6:37 Multiply & Add Obscure
7:39 Using Copy/Paste, the loop bar & Morph

More Videos:

Walkthrough (Main):
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hWJAfd80J68

Obscurium Clock
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tA8DgtkAEK8

Morphing and Shifting
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M3VOIyPLuGY

Plugins
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pLK809abjZE

Thanks to the great Tom Cosm!
http://www.cosm.co.nz
https://www.facebook.com/tomcosmnz?fref=ts
transcript: [Music] welcome to the first video in a four-part series on the more advanced features in Sugar Bites obscurium this video we're going to be covering the draw tools the modifiers and the super obscure mode and we're going to be using the erog get preset which is found in the AOA sub folder right so here's what it sounds like and over to the draw tools now we have a pin tool or a free draw tool we have a line tool which is a uh well it's a ruler tool what draws lines we have a paint brush which is the random generator tool and we have a sign tool so the pin tool is the free draw tool and you can simply click around in the motion sequencer and draw your values in however you like or you can do individual steps we can also change how many steps we want to draw at once with the slider around the edge so for example I could pick eight steps and draw eight steps chunks or I could do four steps and just change four steps at once the line tool or the ruler tool draws a straight line in an angle that you define by using this slider here so I'm going to angle It Up Click at the bottom and drag to get a nice smooth line or I can angle it down click and drag to get a line down you can also draw backwards if you like the paint tool which is the fun one is the random generator this slider determines how much random generation happens so if I have it on a low amount and move it we get a little bit of Randomness but if I put it up to the max we get a big amount very good now the sign tool is a little sine wave generator it's like an Alo in away you pick a frequency or a speed or how fast it goes and then you pick an amplitude which is how intense it is or how much is applied so let's just have a little of both and I'm going to click and drag and you'll see we get a nice sine wave in our motion sequencer um let's use a lot of frequency and a lot of amplitude and you'll see things get quite chaotic we also have a draw all button the draw all button draws all the parameter values at once so we can click uh on a step and draw them all at once but the offset determines the spread or how spaced out they are so we can do big spaced out uh spread draws like that or we can make them small and just have little small gathered parameter values moving over to the modifiers the modifiers is a very interesting area it allow allows you to isolate a single step and then do things to the parameters shift them around copy and paste do all this kind of stuff uh the first two buttons are the move all left button and the move all right button this moves the entire MO sequencer either left or right the lock button is really handy when this is enabled when you pick a step it locks that step in so now if I play a note it's going to continue playing that step over and over if the lock button is off it starts from the start of the sequence so I'm going to keep that on for now right we have here a copy and paste to the left button and a copy and paste to the right button so let's say I pick this step and I click the button button it's copied that step and put it directly to the left or I can do it and click the copy and paste to the right and it's pasted it to the right we also have a swap around with a left or swap around with a right button so if I select this step here click this button it's now swapped these two steps around and I can do it in the other way it swap these two steps around there is a copy and a paste so if I choose copy here uh sorry choose the step and copy it I can then click somewhere else and paste it and I find this is really handy for when you're working with percussion say you make a good snare and you want to copy the snare to the right places this is where you would use the copy and the paste let's talk about the modifiers so we have three modifiers here the first one is the move modifier what this does is if I select a step it moves all the parameter values up or down so every single value gets moved evenly up or down you'll notice as it reaches the top or the bottom of the screen it reverses itself and it kind of mirrors so it it keeps going but it just start going in the opposite direction this is what it sounds like the next modifier is the stretch or Shrink modifier and this shrinks all the values down to one particular point or expands them away this is a really good effect let's listen so I'm going to shrink it down and use the move modifi to bring them all down low and then let's expand it we'll stretch it out bring it back up to the middle and expand it out again the final one is the rotate modifier this is like using the shift slider over here except it's more destructive what it does is it swaps one parameter for the parameter above it or below it so it it swaps the value around so you'll see as I use the move tool all the colorful dots kind of cycle around and all of these values are going to be cycled around let's use them all at once finally there's an all button and this is Handy if you quickly just want to adjust a single parameter so if I turn this off only the parameter that I have have selected will now be applied using these modifiers so this is good if you just quickly want to um change one parameter without having to go back into the draw tools mode you can just turn this off and quickly change one parameter okay let's move over to super obscurer super obscurer is the random generator and what it does is it picks a whole set of new draw tools for each of the 16 parameter modulation lanes and draws a whole new sequence for you and that sequence size depends on how big this grid slider is so if I just have the Grid on one it's going to randomly generate every single step if I have it on eight it's going to wait eight steps before it re randomizes all those draw tools so I'm going to put it at four for now and I'll just turn it on for you and play it for you and I'll explain what happens afterwards let's play it now you'll notice that when that sequence finished it turn the power off for us this is really handy so you don't overwrite stuff that you've just randomly generated you can help however turn on Infinity and what that does is when it reaches the end of the sequence it will continue to randomly generate until you turn it off manually now what's going on with these dials here now we have a multiplier and we have an addition so I'm going to turn this on the multiplier determines the spread over the vertical axis so this determines the spread of all the parameter values and the add determines the position of where the values are going to be so if I make this quite large and this quite down and let's make it every two steps and [Applause] play we can bring this down and make this quite high if we like we also have a random add button and when you turn this on it randomly changes the add value but you can determine how random it is using this slider here so if I pick one grid Point random add on maximum random generation for the for the ad and put the multiplier down uh I will make the that's quite large we're going to get real chaos every single step very good now one thing you can do is if you like a particular segment that you've randomly generated let's say I like this first Loop bar I'm going to bring this um Loop bar back to these first four steps and I'm going to copy it using the morph area that means we can now paste it anywhere we like I can um drag these Loop bars to another area and paste it all or I can drag the loop bars out to expand over the whole motion sequencer and hit paste and those first four steps now are over everything so there we go that concludes this video on draw tools modifier and super obscure
